instruments: Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance (Cap. 615) (proposed amendment, not yet enacted)
standing_summary: The FSTB and SFC ran a joint consultation (27 June to 29 August 2025) on new licensing regimes for virtual asset dealing and custodian services, aligned respectively with the existing Type 1 (dealing in securities) and a dedicated private-key-custody framework. Consultation conclusions were published 24 December 2025, alongside the launch of a further consultation on separate virtual asset advisory (Type 4-aligned) and management (Type 9-aligned) regimes, which closed 23 January 2026; conclusions on that further consultation were published 26 May 2026. All four regimes are proposed as amendments to the Anti-Money Laundering and Counter-Terrorist Financing Ordinance. No deeming or transitional arrangement is planned for any of the four regimes; regulators are instead urging firms to begin pre-application engagement. Licensed dealers will be required to custody client virtual assets with SFC-regulated custodians, with HKMA-licensed stablecoin issuers exempt for their own coins. As of the most recent primary source found (a LegCo background brief dated 28 May 2026), the bill remained a stated target for introduction to the Legislative Council within 2026, not yet introduced.

key_links.label: FSTB/SFC consultation conclusions: VA custodian services (24 Dec 2025)
FSTB/SFC consultation conclusions: VA dealing, and further consultation on VA advisory/management (24 Dec 2025)

LegCo Panel on Financial Affairs background brief (28 May 2026)

open_items: The bill has not been confirmed as gazetted or introduced to LegCo as of the most recent primary source located (28 May 2026); its status for June-July 2026 is unconfirmed.
Detailed regulatory requirements beyond the consultation conclusions' high-level terms are still to be issued.
The commencement date for the new regimes, once enacted, has not yet been set.
A proposed limited self-custody threshold for private-equity/venture-capital fund managers' new tokens remains under consideration, not finalized.
